description:
Fix documentation inaccuracies that cause friction for first-time plugin developers. Three issues: (1) The CLI reference page and an SVG illustration reference the unscoped `create-opentabs-plugin` command which returns a 404 on npm — should reference `@opentabs-dev/create-plugin`. (2) The plugin-development guide mentions that `resources` and `prompts` need `override` but doesn't mention that lifecycle hooks (`onActivate`, `onDeactivate`, `onNavigate`, `onToolInvocationStart`, `onToolInvocationEnd`) also need `override`. A developer implementing hooks gets TS4114 with no guidance from the main guide. (3) The plugin-development guide doesn't warn about `npx opentabs-plugin` 404 when not installed locally — developers should know the scoped package name.

Known LOW issues (not worth stories): The illustration SVG text says `create-opentabs-plugin` in an SVG `<text>` element at docs/components/illustrations.tsx:2715 — this is a cosmetic label in a flow diagram and is low priority.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix unscoped create-opentabs-plugin reference in CLI reference docs
userStories.description: As a plugin developer reading the CLI reference, I want accurate package names so that I don't get a confusing npm 404 error when I try to run the documented command.
acceptanceCriteria: The CLI reference page no longer references the unscoped `create-opentabs-plugin` package name
Line 779 of docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx says `@opentabs-dev/create-plugin` instead of `create-opentabs-plugin`
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: `docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx`, line 779.

Current text: `Or use it as a local dev dependency via \`npm run build\` inside your plugin project (the scaffolded project from \`create-opentabs-plugin\` includes it).`

Change `create-opentabs-plugin` to `@opentabs-dev/create-plugin` (or rephrase to say 'the scaffolded project includes it as a dev dependency').

Also check if there are any other references to the unscoped name in the docs directory: grep for `create-opentabs-plugin` across all docs files. The main guides (plugin-development.mdx, quick-start.mdx, install/index.mdx) already correctly use `@opentabs-dev/create-plugin`, so this is likely the only remaining reference besides the SVG illustration (which is cosmetic and low priority).

userStories.title: Document that lifecycle hooks require the override keyword
userStories.description: As a plugin developer adding lifecycle hooks, I want the plugin-development guide to mention that `onActivate`, `onDeactivate`, `onNavigate`, `onToolInvocationStart`, and `onToolInvocationEnd` require the `override` keyword, so that I don't get a confusing TS4114 error.
acceptanceCriteria: The plugin-development guide (docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx) mentions that lifecycle hooks require `override` — either in the existing paragraph about `override` for resources/prompts (line 94), or in a new section about lifecycle hooks
The mention explains WHY override is needed (TypeScript's noImplicitOverride rule, these are optional methods with defaults on the base class)
The mention lists all 5 hooks that need override: onActivate, onDeactivate, onNavigate, onToolInvocationStart, onToolInvocationEnd
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: `docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx`, around line 94.

The existing paragraph (line 94) says: 'Beyond tools, your plugin can also declare `resources` (read-only data the agent can browse) and `prompts` (reusable message templates). Both require the `override` keyword because they are optional properties with default values on the base class — `tools` is abstract and does not require `override`. See [Resources & Prompts](/docs/guides/resources-prompts) for the full guide.'

This paragraph should be expanded to also mention lifecycle hooks. Suggested addition after the existing text (before the Resources & Prompts link): 'Similarly, lifecycle hooks (`onActivate`, `onDeactivate`, `onNavigate`, `onToolInvocationStart`, `onToolInvocationEnd`) require `override` because they are optional methods defined on the base class. See [Lifecycle Hooks](/docs/sdk/lifecycle-hooks) for usage examples.'

The SDK reference page (docs/content/docs/sdk/lifecycle-hooks.mdx) already shows `override` in its code examples — this story is about making the main developer guide mention it so developers discover it before hitting TS4114.

Also check the resources-prompts guide (docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx) for its full example at line 494-507 — it already uses `override` on everything including hooks. This confirms the pattern is correct but just not documented prominently enough in the main guide.
